# Architecture — Pattern Index

134 patterns. Scan this index to shortlist, then read the linked files. Schema: [`../../SCHEMA.md`](../../SCHEMA.md).

## ai

- [LLM Applications](ai/llm/_index.md) `llm` (metapattern) — A family of patterns for structuring applications built on top of large language models — _llm, generative-ai, agents, rag, prompt, context, tool-use_
- [ML Systems](ai/ml-systems/_index.md) `ml-systems` (metapattern) — A family of patterns for the infrastructure that trains, tracks, and serves machine learning models — _mlops, training, inference, feature-engineering, model-management_
- [Agent](ai/llm/agent.md) `agent` — An LLM in a loop: reasons, calls tools, observes results, and iterates until it reaches an answer or stopping condition — _agent, autonomy, tool-use, planning, reasoning, loop_
- [AI Gateway](ai/llm/ai-gateway.md) `ai-gateway` — Proxy between apps and LLM providers centralizing routing, caching, rate limits, provider fallback, cost tracking, and observability — _gateway, routing, fallback, caching, rate-limiting, multi-provider_
- [Evaluator-Optimizer](ai/llm/evaluator-optimizer.md) `evaluator-optimizer` — Pairs a generator that produces a candidate output with an evaluator that assesses it against criteria and returns actionable feedback — _reflection, evaluation, iteration, self-critique, feedback, workflow_
- [Human-in-the-Loop](ai/llm/human-in-the-loop.md) `human-in-the-loop` — Inserts human review into an LLM workflow where stakes are too high for autonomy — irreversible, low-confidence, or regulated actions — _oversight, review, approval, escalation, safety, confidence_
- [Memory](ai/llm/memory.md) `memory` — Persists information across sessions so the LLM can recall facts, preferences, and prior conversation context beyond what fits in a single context window — _memory, context, persistence, recall, personalization, sessions_
- [Multi-Agent](ai/llm/multi-agent.md) `multi-agent` — Coordinates multiple specialized LLM agents to complete tasks that exceed the capability or context window of a single agent — _multi-agent, orchestration, specialization, parallelism, coordination_
- [Parallelization](ai/llm/parallelization.md) `parallelization` — Runs multiple LLM calls concurrently and aggregates their outputs — _parallelism, sectioning, voting, aggregation, concurrency, workflow_
- [Planning](ai/llm/planning.md) `planning` — Model produces an explicit step decomposition of a goal, then executes it, interleaving reasoning with action (ReAct) — _planning, reasoning, decomposition, react, autonomy, workflow_
- [Prompt Chaining](ai/llm/prompt-chaining.md) `prompt-chaining` — Decomposes a complex task into a sequence of simpler LLM calls, where each call processes the output of the previous one — _chaining, pipeline, decomposition, sequential, workflow_
- [Retrieval-Augmented Generation](ai/llm/rag.md) `rag` — Augments an LLM's generation with documents retrieved at query time from an external knowledge store — _rag, retrieval, vector-search, grounding, knowledge-base, context_
- [Routing](ai/llm/routing.md) `routing` — Classifies an input and directs it to a specialized downstream handler — a dedicated prompt, model, or workflow tuned for that category — _routing, classification, dispatch, specialization, workflow_
- [Tool Use](ai/llm/tool-use.md) `tool-use` — Provides the LLM with a set of typed function signatures it can invoke during generation — _function-calling, tools, actions, integration, extensibility_
- [Feature Store](ai/ml-systems/feature-store.md) `feature-store` — A centralized system for computing, storing, and serving machine learning features consistently across training and inference — _features, training-serving-skew, reuse, consistency, mlops_
- [Model Registry](ai/ml-systems/model-registry.md) `model-registry` — A versioned catalog of trained model artifacts along with their metadata — training metrics, data lineage, hyperparameters, and deployment history — _versioning, promotion, lineage, governance, mlops_
- [Model Serving](ai/ml-systems/model-serving.md) `model-serving` — Loads a trained model and exposes it for inference — online (low-latency per request) or batch (bulk over a dataset) — _inference, serving, online-prediction, batch-prediction, latency_
- [Training Pipeline](ai/ml-systems/training-pipeline.md) `training-pipeline` — An orchestrated, reproducible sequence of steps that transforms raw data into a trained, evaluated model artifact — _training, pipeline, reproducibility, automation, mlops_

## basic

- [Layers](basic/layers/_index.md) `layers` (metapattern) — Organizes a system into horizontal tiers of abstraction — one component per level of abstractness — _abstraction, separation-of-concerns, horizontal-tiers, specialization_
- [Pipeline](basic/pipeline/_index.md) `pipeline` (metapattern) — A system topology where data flows through a chain of processors — one component per step of data transformation — _sequential-processing, data-transformation, composability, stateless-steps_
- [Services](basic/services/_index.md) `services` (metapattern) — Divides a large codebase into independently deployable components, one per subdomain — _decoupling, subdomains, multi-team, independent-deployment, scalability_
- [Shards](basic/shards/_index.md) `shards` (metapattern) — Multiple functionally identical subsystems with little or no intercommunication — _scalability, replication, stateless, fault-tolerance, horizontal-scaling_
- [Monolith](basic/monolith.md) `monolith` — A single deployable unit with no architectural boundaries enforced between components — _single-deployment, simplicity, rapid-development, co-location_
- [Domain-Driven Design](basic/layers/domain-driven-design.md) `domain-driven-design` — Organizes code around the business domain with bounded contexts and a ubiquitous language shared with domain experts — _domain-model, bounded-context, ubiquitous-language, aggregates, value-objects, domain-events_
- [Entity-Control-Boundary](basic/layers/entity-control-boundary.md) `entity-control-boundary` — Three object roles: Entity (domain data), Control (use-case coordination), Boundary (interfaces to external actors) — _use-case, roles, entity, control, boundary, object-oriented, enterprise, embedded_
- [Layered Monolith](basic/layers/layered-monolith.md) `layered-monolith` — A single-deployable application that enforces strict horizontal layer boundaries internally: typically Presentation, Application, Domain, and Infrastructure — _monolith, layers, single-deployable, separation-of-concerns, presentation, application, domain, infrastructure_
- [Three-Tier Architecture](basic/layers/three-tier.md) `three-tier` — Structures an application into three horizontal layers: Presentation (UI), Business Logic (application/domain), and Data Access (persistence) — _web, separation-of-concerns, presentation, business-logic, persistence_
- [Batch Processing](basic/pipeline/batch-processing.md) `batch-processing` — Data is accumulated over a time interval and then processed as a discrete, bounded job — _batch, scheduled, high-throughput, etl, offline, periodic, bounded-data_
- [Data Mesh](basic/pipeline/data-mesh.md) `data-mesh` — Decentralizes data ownership by assigning each domain team responsibility for publishing its data as a versioned, discoverable, and interoperable data product — _data-products, domain-ownership, federated-governance, decentralization, self-serve, analytical-data_
- [Event-Driven Architecture](basic/pipeline/event-driven-architecture.md) `event-driven-architecture` — Components communicate by producing and consuming events through a broker, with no direct dependencies between producers and consumers — _async, decoupling, choreography, event, loose-coupling_
- [Pipes and Filters](basic/pipeline/pipes-and-filters.md) `pipes-and-filters` — Decomposes processing into independent filter stages, each one transformation, passing results downstream via pipes — _pipeline, composition, filters, transformation, unix-philosophy, reusable-stages_
- [Stream Processing](basic/pipeline/stream-processing.md) `stream-processing` — Data is processed continuously as it arrives on an unbounded stream, with results produced in near real-time rather than after a full dataset is accumulated — _real-time, streaming, low-latency, stateful, unbounded-data, continuous-computation_
- [Workflow System](basic/pipeline/workflow-system.md) `workflow-system` — Long-running, multi-step business processes are modeled as explicit, persisted workflows — _workflow, orchestration, long-running, durable, human-tasks, approval, state-machine, bpm_
- [Actors](basic/services/actors.md) `actors` — Each service (or fine-grained unit of logic) is modeled as an actor: an independent entity with its own private state and a message inbox — _async, message-passing, isolation, concurrency, no-shared-state, distributed_
- [Function as a Service](basic/services/faas.md) `faas` — Each discrete unit of business logic is packaged as a stateless function deployed and billed independently — _serverless, stateless, event-driven, auto-scaling, pay-per-use, cloud_
- [Macroservices](basic/services/macroservices.md) `macroservices` — Decomposes the system into two to five large services aligned to major business domains, each owning its data store and deployed independently — _services, coarse-grained, independent-deployment, pragmatic, reduced-overhead_
- [Microservices](basic/services/microservices.md) `microservices` — The system is decomposed into small, independently deployable services, each owning its own data store and communicating over the network — _independent-deployment, distributed, scalability, bounded-context_
- [Modular Monolith](basic/services/modular-monolith.md) `modular-monolith` — A single-deployable application partitioned into well-defined modules that expose explicit public APIs while hiding internal implementation — _monolith, modules, encapsulation, single-deployable, explicit-api, enforced-boundaries_
- [Service-Based Architecture](basic/services/service-based-architecture.md) `service-based-architecture` — Decomposes the system into a small number of independently deployable, coarse-grained services — typically four to twelve — that often share a common database — _services, coarse-grained, shared-database, independent-deployment, pragmatic-decomposition_
- [Actors](basic/shards/actors.md) `actors` — Each actor is an independent, lightweight state machine that owns its private state and communicates exclusively via asynchronous messages — _isolation, concurrency, stateful, message-passing, distributed-state_
- [Autoscaling](basic/shards/autoscaling.md) `autoscaling` — Adjusts the number of running instances automatically in response to load, adding capacity when demand rises and removing it when demand falls — _elasticity, horizontal-scaling, capacity, cost, stateless, resilience_
- [Cells](basic/shards/cells.md) `cells` — Each shard is a fully self-contained replica of the entire system stack — routing, logic, and data — serving a fixed partition of users or tenants — _isolation, blast-radius, resilience, multi-tenant, horizontal-scaling_
- [Geode](basic/shards/geode.md) `geode` — Deploys a complete, independent copy of the workload (a "geode" — geographical node) to multiple regions, each able to serve any request — _geo-distribution, multi-region, availability, latency, active-active, resilience_
- [Lambdas](basic/shards/lambdas.md) `lambdas` — Ephemeral single-use compute instances provisioned on demand, each executing exactly one task before being destroyed — _serverless, ephemeral, event-driven, per-invocation, elasticity_
- [Multitenancy](basic/shards/multitenancy.md) `multitenancy` — A single shared instance of the application and its infrastructure serves multiple tenants simultaneously — _isolation, cost-efficiency, saas, logical-separation, shared-resources_
- [Partitions](basic/shards/partitions.md) `partitions` — Data is divided into non-overlapping horizontal slices distributed across multiple nodes — _data-sharding, scalability, key-range, distributed-data, horizontal-partitioning_
- [Stateless Pool](basic/shards/stateless-pool.md) `stateless-pool` — Identical stateless instances behind a load balancer; any instance handles any request since no request-local state is kept — _stateless, horizontal-scaling, load-balancing, resilience, elasticity_
- [Work Queue](basic/shards/work-queue.md) `work-queue` — A pool of identical worker instances pulls tasks from a shared, durable queue — _async, backpressure, durability, horizontal-scaling, decoupling_

## extension

- [Middleware](extension/middleware/_index.md) `middleware` (metapattern) — An intermediary component between services that handles routing, transformation, and delivery of messages — _async, decoupling, integration, broker, routing_
- [Orchestrator](extension/orchestrator/_index.md) `orchestrator` (metapattern) — A central coordinator that knows the steps of a workflow and explicitly calls each participant service in the correct sequence — _workflow, coordination, control-flow, distributed, saga_
- [Proxy](extension/proxy/_index.md) `proxy` (metapattern) — An intermediary between client and component that forwards requests, optionally adding routing, caching, auth, or observability — _proxy, routing, cross-cutting, interception, transparency_
- [Sandwich](extension/sandwich.md) `sandwich` — Shared infrastructure layer between two service layers — presentation/API services on top, data/domain services below — _layers, infrastructure, separation-of-concerns, tiered, fragmented_
- [Shared Repository](extension/shared-repository.md) `shared-repository` — A single shared datastore that multiple services read from and write to directly — _data, integration, coupling, consistency, persistence_
- [Claim-Check](extension/middleware/claim-check.md) `claim-check` — Splits a large message so the bulk payload is stored externally and only a small reference (the claim check) travels through the messaging system — _messaging, payload, offloading, storage, performance, decoupling_
- [Enterprise Service Bus](extension/middleware/enterprise-service-bus.md) `enterprise-service-bus` — A centralized middleware platform that provides message routing, data transformation, protocol mediation, and orchestration across enterprise systems — _integration, transformation, orchestration, protocol-mediation, enterprise_
- [Event Mediator](extension/middleware/event-mediator.md) `event-mediator` — A central component that receives events and orchestrates a multi-step workflow by calling services in a defined sequence — _events, orchestration, workflow, coordination, mediation_
- [Message Broker](extension/middleware/message-broker.md) `message-broker` — A dedicated service that receives messages from producers, routes them to the correct consumers, and optionally persists them until delivery is confirmed — _messaging, async, decoupling, routing, queuing_
- [Persistent Event Log](extension/middleware/persistent-event-log.md) `persistent-event-log` — An append-only, ordered log of events that retains all events durably for a configurable retention period (or indefinitely) — _event-sourcing, append-only, replay, durability, streaming_
- [Queue-Based Load Leveling](extension/middleware/queue-based-load-leveling.md) `queue-based-load-leveling` — Places a queue between producers and a consuming service so the consumer processes work at its own steady rate regardless of how bursty the incoming load is — _messaging, buffering, load-leveling, decoupling, resilience, throughput_
- [Service Mesh](extension/middleware/service-mesh.md) `service-mesh` — Sidecar proxies alongside each service handle inter-service traffic: load balancing, retries, circuit breaking, mTLS, tracing — _sidecar, observability, mtls, traffic-management, resilience_
- [API Composer](extension/orchestrator/api-composer.md) `api-composer` — Aggregates responses from multiple downstream services into a single composed response for the client — _aggregation, fan-out, composition, client-optimization, orchestration_
- [Choreography](extension/orchestrator/choreography.md) `choreography` — Coordinates a workflow without a central controller — services react to and emit events; the process emerges from local reactions — _choreography, events, decentralized, coordination, decoupling, workflow_
- [Facade](extension/orchestrator/facade.md) `facade` — A simplified interface to a complex subsystem — _simplification, abstraction, interface, subsystem, encapsulation_
- [MapReduce](extension/orchestrator/mapreduce.md) `mapreduce` — Orchestrator distributes work across nodes (Map: independent per-partition work) then aggregates results (Reduce) — _parallel, distributed, data-processing, batch, aggregation_
- [Saga](extension/orchestrator/saga.md) `saga` — Manages a long-running distributed transaction as a sequence of local transactions, where each step publishes an event or message that triggers the next step — _distributed-transactions, compensation, consistency, resilience, workflow_
- [Service Layer](extension/orchestrator/service-layer.md) `service-layer` — A set of operations available to the application that defines the system's boundary and coordinates the application's response to each use case — _application-layer, coordination, boundary, use-case, transactions_
- [Transaction Script](extension/orchestrator/transaction-script.md) `transaction-script` — Business logic is organized as a single procedural script per use case that runs through a series of steps from start to finish — _procedural, simplicity, use-case, scripting, business-logic_
- [Adapter](extension/proxy/adapter.md) `adapter` — Converts one component's interface to what another expects, letting incompatible components work together unmodified — _interface, compatibility, translation, integration, structural_
- [Ambassador](extension/proxy/ambassador.md) `ambassador` — A specialized sidecar that acts as an outbound proxy for a service, handling all egress calls on that service's behalf — _sidecar, outbound, resilience, circuit-breaker, protocol-translation_
- [Anticorruption Layer](extension/proxy/anticorruption-layer.md) `anticorruption-layer` — A translation layer that insulates a clean domain model from a legacy or external system's messy, inconsistent, or semantically incompatible model — _ddd, translation, isolation, legacy, domain-model_
- [API Gateway](extension/proxy/api-gateway.md) `api-gateway` — Single entry point in front of backend services handling auth, rate limiting, routing, SSL termination, and transformation — _routing, auth, rate-limiting, ingress, aggregation_
- [Caching Layer](extension/proxy/caching-layer.md) `caching-layer` — A proxy that intercepts requests destined for a backend service and serves responses from a local cache when a valid cached entry exists — _caching, performance, read-throughput, latency, offloading_
- [Edge Service](extension/proxy/edge-service.md) `edge-service` — Proxy at the network edge (CDN PoP near users) serving content with minimal latency by avoiding origin round-trips — _cdn, edge, latency, geographic, offloading_
- [Load Balancer](extension/proxy/load-balancer.md) `load-balancer` — A proxy that distributes incoming requests across a pool of backend instances using a configured algorithm (round-robin, least-connections, IP hash, etc.) — _scalability, availability, distribution, failover, traffic_
- [Presentation Layer](extension/proxy/presentation-layer.md) `presentation-layer` — Renders data in the format each consumer requires — HTML for browsers, JSON for API clients, XML for legacy integrations — _ui, rendering, separation-of-concerns, consumer-facing, transformation_
- [Reverse Proxy](extension/proxy/reverse-proxy.md) `reverse-proxy` — A proxy that sits in front of one or more backend servers and forwards client requests to them — _ingress, ssl-termination, routing, infrastructure, transparency_
- [Sidecar](extension/proxy/sidecar.md) `sidecar` — Helper container/process co-deployed with a service adding logging, config, secrets, or local proxying without code changes — _sidecar, co-deployment, cross-cutting, container, extensibility_
- [Static Content Hosting](extension/proxy/static-content-hosting.md) `static-content-hosting` — Serves static assets — images, scripts, stylesheets, downloads — directly from a storage service or CDN edge instead of routing them through the application — _cdn, static-content, offloading, latency, performance, edge_

## fragmented

- [Hierarchy](fragmented/hierarchy/_index.md) `hierarchy` (metapattern) — Services organized into strict levels — upper layers delegate downward; lower layers never call upward — _hierarchical, abstraction, delegation, layering, dependency-direction_
- [Layered Services](fragmented/layered-services/_index.md) `layered-services` (metapattern) — Combines Layers and Services — independent services, each internally structured into horizontal layers — _layered, services, decomposition, separation-of-concerns, modularity_
- [Polyglot Persistence](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/_index.md) `polyglot-persistence` (metapattern) — Different data stores for different parts of the system, each chosen to fit that component's data shape and access patterns — _multi-database, data-stores, heterogeneous, storage, scalability_
- [Service-Oriented Architecture](fragmented/service-oriented-architecture/_index.md) `service-oriented-architecture` (metapattern) — Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) is an architectural style in which software components are published, discovered, and consumed as services — _soa, services, loose-coupling, interoperability, enterprise_
- [Backends for Frontends](fragmented/backends-for-frontends.md) `backends-for-frontends` — A dedicated backend per client type (web, mobile, TV) instead of one general-purpose API for all clients — _bff, api-gateway, client-specific, aggregation, frontend_
- [Cell-Based Architecture](fragmented/hierarchy/cell-based-architecture.md) `cell-based-architecture` — Cell-Based Architecture organizes services into self-contained, independently deployable cells — _cells, fault-isolation, scalability, routing, resilience_
- [CQRS](fragmented/layered-services/cqrs.md) `cqrs` — Separates the write model (commands) from the read model (queries) into distinct components and optionally datastores — _read-write-separation, scalability, query-optimization, event-sourcing_
- [Cache-Aside](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/cache-aside.md) `cache-aside` — Application code checks the cache first before reading from the database — _caching, lazy-loading, read-performance, resilience_
- [CQRS View Database](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/cqrs-view.md) `cqrs-view` — A CQRS View Database is a dedicated read-optimized datastore (or schema) maintained as a projection of the command-side's events or state changes — _cqrs, read-model, projection, eventual-consistency, query-optimization_
- [Data Lake](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/data-lake.md) `data-lake` — Centralized repository storing raw data in native format — structured or not — with no predefined schema at write time — _analytics, schema-on-read, raw-data, unstructured, exploratory_
- [Data Warehouse](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/data-warehouse.md) `data-warehouse` — A Data Warehouse is a central repository of integrated data from multiple operational sources, optimized for analytical queries (OLAP) — _analytics, olap, etl, reporting, schema-on-write_
- [Event Sourcing](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/event-sourcing.md) `event-sourcing` — State is derived by replaying an append-only sequence of domain events rather than storing the current state directly — _event-log, append-only, audit-trail, temporal, cqrs_
- [Materialized View](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/materialized-view.md) `materialized-view` — A Materialized View is a pre-computed, stored query result that is refreshed when the underlying source data changes — _precomputed, query-performance, caching, consistency, aggregation_
- [Read Replicas](fragmented/polyglot-persistence/read-replicas.md) `read-replicas` — Read Replicas are read-only copies of a primary database, kept in sync via continuous replication — _replication, scalability, read-performance, high-availability, eventual-consistency_
- [Distributed Monolith](fragmented/service-oriented-architecture/distributed-monolith.md) `distributed-monolith` — An architecture that appears distributed (multiple deployed services) but retains the tight coupling of a monolith — _anti-pattern, tight-coupling, distributed, fragile_

## implementation

- [Hexagonal Architecture](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/_index.md) `hexagonal-architecture` (metapattern) — Hexagonal Architecture, also known as Ports and Adapters, structures an application so that the domain core is completely isolated from external dependencies — _ports-adapters, testability, domain-isolation, infrastructure, decoupling_
- [Mesh](implementation/mesh/_index.md) `mesh` (metapattern) — The Mesh pattern organizes all nodes in a system as peers that can communicate directly with any other node without routing through a central coordinator — _peer-to-peer, decentralized, distributed, resilience, scalability_
- [Microkernel](implementation/microkernel/_index.md) `microkernel` (metapattern) — The Microkernel pattern organizes a system around a minimal, stable core that provides only the essential services needed to load and coordinate plug-in modules — _extensibility, modularity, plug-in, minimal-core, flexibility_
- [Plugins](implementation/plugins.md) `plugins` — The Plugin metapattern allows a core system to be extended with additional functionality at runtime or build time without modifying the core — _extensibility, runtime-extension, interface, discovery, modularity_
- [Clean Architecture](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/clean-architecture.md) `clean-architecture` — Concentric rings — Entities, Use Cases, Interface Adapters, Frameworks — with a strict inward-only dependency rule — _dependency-inversion, testability, domain-isolation, ports-adapters_
- [Model-View-Presenter](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/model-view-presenter.md) `model-view-presenter` — Model-View-Presenter is a derivative of MVC in which the View is completely passive — it contains no logic, only renders what it is told — _ui-pattern, passive-view, testability, separation-of-concerns, presenter_
- [Model-View-Controller](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/mvc.md) `mvc` — Separates Model (data and rules), View (presentation), and Controller (input handling and coordination) — _separation-of-concerns, ui-pattern, web, desktop, request-response_
- [Model-View-ViewModel](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/mvvm.md) `mvvm` — Model-View-ViewModel is a UI architectural pattern derived from MVC for data-binding-heavy platforms — _data-binding, reactive, ui-pattern, testability, separation-of-concerns_
- [Onion Architecture](implementation/hexagonal-architecture/onion-architecture.md) `onion-architecture` — Concentric layers — Domain Model, Domain Services, Application Services, Infrastructure/UI — with inward-only dependencies — _dependency-inversion, domain-isolation, layering, testability, ports-adapters_
- [Space-Based Architecture](implementation/mesh/space-based-architecture.md) `space-based-architecture` — Space-Based Architecture eliminates the database as a central bottleneck by distributing both processing and data across a grid of identical processing units — _scalability, in-memory-grid, distributed-data, elasticity, high-availability_
- [Container Orchestrator](implementation/microkernel/container-orchestrator.md) `container-orchestrator` — Microkernel managing containerized workload lifecycle: scheduling, scaling, networking, storage, restarting failed instances — _orchestration, containers, scheduling, lifecycle-management, scalability_
- [Domain-Specific Language](implementation/microkernel/dsl.md) `dsl` — A Domain-Specific Language is a specialized mini-language tailored to a narrow problem domain — _dsl, expressiveness, domain-modeling, abstraction, language_
- [Software Framework](implementation/microkernel/software-framework.md) `software-framework` — A Software Framework is a reusable, extensible application skeleton that provides inversion of control: the framework calls user code rather than the reverse — _inversion-of-control, extensibility, hooks, callbacks, reusability_

## operational

- [Deployment](operational/deployment/_index.md) `deployment` (metapattern) — A family of patterns for releasing changes to production systems safely, incrementally, and with controlled blast radius — _release, rollout, rollback, progressive-delivery, risk-reduction_
- [Observability](operational/observability/_index.md) `observability` (metapattern) — A family of patterns for gaining visibility into the internal state of a running distributed system through its external outputs — traces, logs, and metrics — _visibility, telemetry, tracing, logging, metrics, debugging_
- [Resilience](operational/resilience/_index.md) `resilience` (metapattern) — A family of patterns that protect a system's availability when downstream components fail or are overloaded — _fault-tolerance, reliability, availability, degraded-mode, protection_
- [Blue/Green Deployment](operational/deployment/blue-green.md) `blue-green` — Maintains two identical production environments — Blue (currently live) and Green (new version staged) — _zero-downtime, rollback, parallel-environments, cutover_
- [Canary Release](operational/deployment/canary-release.md) `canary-release` — Gradually shifts a small percentage of production traffic to the new version while the majority continues on the stable version — _progressive-delivery, risk-reduction, traffic-splitting, rollout_
- [Feature Flags](operational/deployment/feature-flags.md) `feature-flags` — Wraps new or experimental code paths behind a conditional check against a runtime configuration value — _progressive-delivery, trunk-based-development, dark-launch, kill-switch_
- [Shadow Deployment](operational/deployment/shadow-deployment.md) `shadow-deployment` — Routes a copy of real production traffic to the new version in parallel with the primary version — _validation, risk-reduction, traffic-mirroring, production-testing_
- [Strangler Fig](operational/deployment/strangler-fig.md) `strangler-fig` — Incrementally replaces a legacy system by routing migrated functionality through a facade while the legacy serves the rest — _legacy-migration, incremental-replacement, proxy, coexistence_
- [Distributed Tracing](operational/observability/distributed-tracing.md) `distributed-tracing` — Propagates a unique trace identifier through every service call in a request's path, recording timing and metadata at each hop as a span — _tracing, latency, debugging, span, trace-id, correlation_
- [Health Check](operational/observability/health-check.md) `health-check` — Exposes a dedicated endpoint that reports whether a service instance is alive and able to serve traffic — _availability, orchestration, load-balancer, liveness, readiness_
- [Log Aggregation](operational/observability/log-aggregation.md) `log-aggregation` — Collects log output from all service instances into a single queryable store, rather than leaving logs scattered across individual host filesystems — _logging, centralization, structured-logs, correlation, debugging_
- [Metrics Collection](operational/observability/metrics-collection.md) `metrics-collection` — Services emit numeric time-series measurements — rates, latencies, utilization — aggregated for dashboards and alerting — _metrics, slo, alerting, time-series, RED, USE, golden-signals_
- [Bulkhead](operational/resilience/bulkhead.md) `bulkhead` — Divides a system's resources — threads, connections, memory — into isolated pools, one per downstream dependency or workload class — _fault-isolation, resource-pools, availability, blast-radius_
- [Circuit Breaker](operational/resilience/circuit-breaker.md) `circuit-breaker` — Wraps calls to a downstream dependency in a state machine — _fault-tolerance, cascading-failure, self-healing, availability_
- [Fallback](operational/resilience/fallback.md) `fallback` — Returns an alternative response when the primary path fails — cached data, a default, or reduced functionality instead of an error — _degraded-mode, availability, user-experience, fault-tolerance_
- [Rate Limiting](operational/resilience/rate-limiting.md) `rate-limiting` — Enforces an upper bound on the rate of requests a client or workload class may make within a time window — _capacity-protection, fairness, overload, token-bucket, leaky-bucket_
- [Retry](operational/resilience/retry.md) `retry` — Automatically re-attempts a failed request after a delay, on the assumption that the failure is transient — _transient-failure, fault-tolerance, backoff, jitter_
- [Timeout](operational/resilience/timeout.md) `timeout` — Places an upper bound on how long a call to a downstream component is allowed to take — _latency, fail-fast, resource-release, availability_

## security

- [Security](security/_index.md) `security` (metapattern) — A family of architectural patterns for structuring how trust is established, how authorization is enforced, and how systems behave when security controls fail — _trust, authorization, isolation, identity, access-control_
- [Claims-Based Identity](security/claims-based-identity.md) `claims-based-identity` — Represents identity as signed, portable claims about who the caller is and what they may do, issued by a trusted provider — _identity, authorization, tokens, portability, decentralization_
- [Fail Secure](security/fail-secure.md) `fail-secure` — When a security control fails, default to the most restrictive safe state (deny) rather than open access — _failure-mode, default-deny, authorization, safe-failure_
- [Federated Identity](security/federated-identity.md) `federated-identity` — Delegates authentication to a trusted external identity provider rather than each application managing credentials itself — _authentication, identity, federation, sso, identity-provider, trust_
- [Gateway Authentication](security/gateway-authentication.md) `gateway-authentication` — Centralizes authentication at a gateway or edge component so individual services do not each implement it — _authentication, gateway, offloading, edge, centralization, cross-cutting_
- [Sandboxing](security/sandboxing.md) `sandboxing` — Runs untrusted code in a constrained environment restricting filesystem, network, syscalls, and memory to a defined API surface — _isolation, untrusted-code, blast-radius, containment, execution_
- [Security Zones](security/security-zones.md) `security-zones` — Divides a system into distinct areas with explicitly defined trust levels and controlled communication paths between them — _segmentation, blast-radius, trust-boundary, isolation, dmz_
- [Service Identity](security/service-identity.md) `service-identity` — Gives each workload a cryptographically verifiable identity so services authenticate each other rather than trusting the network — _authentication, service-to-service, mtls, workload-identity, zero-trust, spiffe_
- [Valet Key](security/valet-key.md) `valet-key` — Issues a scoped, time-limited token granting direct restricted access to a resource, bypassing the application per request — _authentication, authorization, delegation, scoped-access, tokens, least-privilege_
- [Zero Trust](security/zero-trust.md) `zero-trust` — Eliminates implicit trust based on network location — no request is trusted because it originates from inside the corporate network or a "trusted" zone — _trust, identity, authorization, network, least-privilege_
